title: Dashboard layout — top bar with username dropdown
description: Build the dashboard page shell with a sticky top bar that contains the + button on the left, the app name 'HeavyDeets' centered, and the user's display name on the right with a dropdown menu.
acceptanceCriteria: Top bar is sticky and visible at all times while scrolling
App name 'HeavyDeets' is centered in the top bar
User's display name appears on the right side of the top bar
Clicking the username opens a dropdown with 'Account Info' and 'Logout' options
+ button appears on the left side of the top bar

steps.details: Create src/web/src/pages/DashboardPage.tsx. Layout: full-height flex column div. Top bar: fixed/sticky header with flex justify-between items-center, padding px-4 py-3, border-bottom, background white. Left slot: + button (placeholder for now). Center slot: 'HeavyDeets' text. Right slot: username dropdown.

Use shadcn/ui DropdownMenu components: DropdownMenuTrigger wrapping a Button variant='ghost' showing user.name, DropdownMenuContent with DropdownMenuItem for 'Account Info' (navigates to /account using useNavigate) and DropdownMenuItem for 'Logout' (calls auth.logout()). Add lucide-react ChevronDown icon next to name.
Below the top bar, add a main content area with flex-1 overflow-y-auto and padding. This will hold the chart and table in later tasks. For now, render placeholder text.

Log in, navigate to /dashboard. Take screenshot. Verify top bar layout. Click username — verify dropdown opens with correct options. Click 'Logout' — verify redirect to /login. Click 'Account Info' — verify navigation to /account.

technicalNotes: Use useAuth() to get user.name and logout() function
shadcn/ui DropdownMenu requires DropdownMenuTrigger, DropdownMenuContent, DropdownMenuItem in that structure
